programmes.show_description: This episode focuses on families' strong bonds and the challenge of breaking bad news. Bill, 92, has a swollen leg and is struggling to breathe. Andy, 45, has stomach cramps.
A motorcycle collision on the M25 creates chaos. A runner has a cardiac arrest during a 5k park run. And a man's fallen down some stairs and fractured his femur.
This episode focuses on families' strong bonds and the challenge of breaking bad news. Bill, 92, has a swollen leg and is struggling to breathe. Andy, 45, has stomach cramps.
This week's camping connoisseurs start their tour of the Highlands at Aberfeldy Caravan Park. They kick things off with a ceilidh and make a splash whitewater rafting.
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
David and Debbie are looking for a holiday home in and around the town of Coin in Spain's Málaga Province. Can they find a place big enough to house their five children and their horse?
Jacqueline and Lynne are hoping to find their dream holiday home in the town of Roquetas de Mar, on Spain's Almería coastline. Will any of Ben Hillman's property picks impress them?
Semi-retired children's nurse Tracey is looking for a holiday home in La Duquesa on Spain's Costa del Sol. Property expert Jasmine Harman suggests five properties for her £140,000 budget.
Jean Johansson helps Andrea and Gary find their dream holiday home on the Caribbean island of St Lucia. What gems do they find for £205k? And are any of them the one?
The week kicks off in Monmouthshire, South Wales, at Kenny and Ben's Creates Monmouth B&B. The guests are wowed by the wild interiors, but one of them worries about a lack of privacy.
Visit two is to Coastal Park Accommodation in Llanelli, run by Claire. Some cleanliness concerns leave one pair of guests quibbling, and there's a fiery exchange over evening drinks.
Billy and Tracy host the third visit, at The Queens at Horton pub and B&B in Shropshire. Former police officer Billy thinks that anything less than perfection is a cop-out...
The final visit is to Whychurch Farm Glamping in Wiltshire, where host Caroline gets her guests to muck in on the farm. But the DIY breakfast hampers make for some not-so-happy glampers.
On payment day, the B&Bers argue about alarms and clash over cleanliness before the payments are revealed and the best value B&B of the week is crowned.
Norwich. Mind reader and magician the 'Amazing' Anthony hopes to wow his guests with dishes close to his heart. Does he have the magic touch? (Ep1/5)
Norwich. Cleaner Hannah hopes to wipe the floor with her rivals, with a night of glitz, glam and great grub. Can she sweep up the £1000 prize? (Ep2/5)
Norwich. Service manager and Adele superfan Jess plans a menu of nutritious nosh, and asks everyone to come dressed as their favourite singer. But will her guests go easy on her? (Ep3/5)
Norwich. Hairdresser Sarah's confident that her menu of family favourites will be a cut above the rest. But will ghostly conversations make her guests curl up and dye? (Ep4/5)
Norwich. Surgeon and foodie Luke hopes to impress with a menu full of flavour, quality and skill. But will his scallops, Sri Lankan curry and tarte Tatin be too much to stomach? (Ep5/5)
Ian and Sally welcome their rivals to N'ista Boutique Rooms in Birkdale on Merseyside. Will a trip to the local lawnmower museum be a hit with their guests?
Mark and Sean host at Oakleigh Guest House in Blackpool, where their USP is value for money. Breakfast goes down well for all, but one pair of guests feel the squeeze in their room.
After an enforced break due to Covid, the competition resumes at Bay House in Scarborough. But breakfast confusion dents host Linda's scores, leaving her questioning her rivals' motives.
Mum and daughter Suzie and Dani host at The Farrier in Cayton, North Yorkshire. Their luxurious rooms go down well with their guests, but issues arise at bedtime.
It's the final day of the competition and the B&Bers have scores to settle. After some floral feedback and plenty of tears, the payments are revealed and the winner is announced.
It's a busy day at King's, as 400 patients are treated in 24 hours. Peggy, who's 85, comes in with her doting husband. And Ben's swollen finger is more serious than he realises.
This episode focuses on mums. Eddie, who's 12, has been hit by a car. It's his fourth visit to A&E on a blue light. Meanwhile, Linda, who's 64, has had respiratory failure.
Charlie has life-threatening injuries after coming off his dirt bike. And anxious parents can only watch and wait as medics treat their five-year-old, who's having seizures.
Heli-medics rush to the scene of a dirt buggy crash in the High Peak district, and treat a farm worker who's had an accident with a potato harvester.
It's a busy day at King's, as 400 patients are treated in 24 hours. Peggy, who's 85, comes in with her doting husband. And Ben's swollen finger is more serious than he realises.
This episode focuses on mums. Eddie, who's 12, has been hit by a car. It's his fourth visit to A&E on a blue light. Meanwhile, Linda, who's 64, has had respiratory failure.
Continuing their tour of the Scottish Highlands, the campers soak up some history and feed the deer on the 145,000-acre Atholl Estate at Blair Castle Caravan Park.
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Leeds area. Choir teacher Dionne serves up a menu that mixes European and Caribbean flavours, with a carnival flourish for good measure. Does it hit the right notes for her guests? (Ep1/5)
Leeds area. Finance director Jane plans a fine dining menu of homemade ravioli, pork and panna cotta. But Denver is still upset about Jane calling her a goth. (Ep2/5)
Leeds area. Drama teacher and DJ Denver aims to bring a taste of Ibiza to Yorkshire, with a Spanish menu, Prosecco van and stilt walkers. But Darren finds it all a bit cringeworthy. (Ep3/5)
Leeds area. Local legend Billy serves up tried-and-tested dinner party favourites. And the best is yet to come at the end of the night with a Frank Sinatra impersonator! (Ep4/5)
Leeds area. Darren plans to put a fancy twist on an apparently basic menu. But Billy is concerned about how much mustard Darren is using. (Ep5/5)
Mark and Caroline want a bolthole on Spain's stunning Orihuela Costa that works for all the family and is close to the golf courses. Scarlette Douglas shows them what their £100k can buy.
Janet and Andrew want to retire to the Spanish sunshine of the western Costa del Sol. Can Danni Menzies make their dream a reality with five impressive properties for their budget of £200k?
Christine and Howard have decided to fulfil their dream of buying a place in the historic town of Mojácar in Spain's Almería region. Can Lee Juggurnauth find the perfect place for £50k?
Hazel's looking for a holiday home on Málaga's east coast. With a budget of £250k and her good friend Paula in tow, can Scarlette Douglas find the perfect place for Hazel?
Jo and DP look for a holiday home in Nerja on Spain's ever-popular Costa del Sol, to escape to in the winter and rent out in the summer. Can Ben Hillman deliver their dream?

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: In Alnwick, Kirstie and Phil meet ministers Chris and Caroline, who need a divine intervention. Caroline loves their home and neighbours, but Chris craves more space.
...Renovations: In Laceby, Matt's on a mission to convert a derelict bakery in his back garden into a home for his sister and nephew. Will his tight budget of £50k be enough dough?
The competition kicks off at The Bickford Arms in Holsworthy, Devon, where self-confessed rookie Keith hopes that his hard work and charisma will impress his more experienced rivals.
Abby Norton hosts at Newchester Farmhouse B&B in Merriott, Somerset. The superstar beds and lacklustre linen receive mixed reactions, but all the guests are wowed by Abby's hospitality.
Collette and Danny hope to impress their rivals at The Quay Inn in Minehead, Somerset. But will cleanliness concerns, a damaged bath panel and sausage squabbles scupper their chances?
Justin and Mike host the final visit at Old Walls Vineyard Luxury Lodges in Bishopsteignton, Devon. The stunning location captivates Abby, but breakfast leaves Danny egg-specting more...
The B&Bers meet for the last time to settle some scores. After a week featuring Wi-Fi woes and a battle of the bangers, the payments are revealed and the winner is announced.
Brand new series: At the Royal Melbourne Hospital, a motorcyclist can't feel his legs after a crash. And a student evacuated out of the Middle East has devastating shrapnel wounds.
Stacie, 38, has fallen out of a tree onto her head. Ashleigh, who's 19, is rushed in after being involved in a high-speed, head-on car crash. And Peter, 78, has abdominal pains.
This episode of the award-winning show, filmed on Red Nose Day, focuses on patients and staff at King's reflecting on the importance of looking on the brighter side of life.
